original: Nicole Stevenson swims world record 200m backstroke (2:06.78)
First surface photos of Pluto are taken by the Hubble Space Telescope
Baseball exhibition season opens with semi-pro & amateur umpires
Canuck's Ron Sedlbauer fails on 5th penalty shot against Islanders
Jim Abbott, 1-handed pitcher, wins 58th James E Sullivan Award as the top amateur athlete in the US, becoming 1st baseball player to win the award
Norwegian explorerRoald Amundsenannounces his team was the first to reach the South Pole on December 14, 1911, 34 days before British explorerRobert Falcon Scott
Malcolm Campbellsets world land speed record speed of 276.71 mph driving his famous Blue Bird car; last record set at Daytona Beach, Florida
Despite being only 17 years old, Brazilian soccer starNeymarmakes his professional debut for Santos
Nikola Jokićmakes NBA history recording the league's first ever 30-point, 20-rebound, 20-assist game as his Denver Nuggets beat the Phoenix Suns 149-141 OT
Stanley Miller, American chemist (created 'primordial soup' experiment to show how life might have originated on earth), born in Oakland, California (d. 2007)
Janet Guthrie, American auto racer (1st woman to race in Indianapolis 500 & Daytona 500; first woman to lead a lap in NASCAR Winston Cup Series), born in Iowa City
Jarmilla Kratochvilova run world record 400 m indoor (49.59 sec)
"USS Monitor", Union Ship sunk in 1862 during US Civil War, restored at Cape Hatteras
Gavaskar becomes 1st cricket batsman to score 10,000 Test runs
Partial eclipse of the Sun (Hawaii, NW North America, Greenland)
Malta's famous landmark the Azure Window collapses into the sea after a storm
First nonstop flight made from America to Asia across the Bering Strait (Nome to North Cape) byNoel Wienand Calvin Cripe for Wien Air Alaska
Wayne Gretzkybreaks own NHL season record with 136th assist
Arthur Conan Doylepublishes "The Return of Sherlock Holmes" collection in London after public pressure to revive his famous detective (Feb New York)
The Royal Institution of Great Britain founded; dedicated to scientific research and education.
1st cadets graduated from flying school at Tuskegee, Alabama
Mike Tysonbeats James 'Bonecrusher' Smith by unanimous decision in 12 rounds in Las Vegas for WBC / WBA heavyweight boxing titles
Alexander Graham Bellreceives a patent for the telephone in the US
Bobo Holloman, American baseball pitcher (no-hitter on MLB debut 1953; St. Louis Browns), born in Thomaston, Georgia (d. 1987)
Michael Rosbash, American geneticist (Nobel Prize 2017, Drosophila period gene), born in Kansas City, Missouri
First jazz record is released on a 78 by the Original Dixieland Jass Band for the Victor Talking Machine Company ("Dixie Jazz Band One Step" on one side, "Livery Stable Blues" on the other)
Earthquake measuring 8 on Richter scale strikes Tango, Japan
South African cricket opening batsman Jackie McGlew scores an unbeaten 255 in 1st Test win against New Zealand in Wellington
Russia wins their 1st international ice hockey competition
Launch of OSO 1, 1st astronomy satellite (solar flare data)
Mass protest outside the National Assembly of Kuwait building for women's voting rights in Kuwait
Ezzard CharlesbeatsJersey Joe Walcottin 15 for 2nd time to win National Boxing Association world heavyweight title, at the Olympia in Detroit, Michigan
Musical "Peter Pan", starringMary Martinbroadcast live on NBC as part of "Producers' Showcase" series; attracts a then record 65 million viewers

RCA releases "Young Americans",David Bowie's 9th studio album, recorded in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and New York City; featuring appearances byJohn Lennonon two tracks, it peaks in the U.S. charts at No. 9, and No. 2 in the U.K.
Whitcomb Judson, American inventor (zipper), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1909)
Henri Landwirth, Belgian-American hotel owner and philanthropist (Give Kids the World), born in Antwerp, Belgium (d. 2018)
Viktor Savinykh, Soviet-Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz T-4, T-13, TM-5), born in Berezkiny, Kirov, Russia
J.R. Richard, American baseball pitcher (MLB All Star 1980; NL ERA leader 1979; NL strikeout leader 1978, 79; Houston Astros), born in Vienna, Louisiana (d. 2021)
Cincinnati Red Stockings, 1st pro BB team, begin 8-mo tour of Midwest & East
Battle at Gura: Ethiopian emperor Yohannes beats Egyptians
The Beatles made their broadcasting debut on BBC radio
CaptainJames Cook1st sights Oregon coast at Yaquina Bay
Edgar Evans, Welsh explorer (part of Robert Scott's Terra Nova Expedition to Antarctica), born in Rhossili, Wales (d. 1912)
Jackie Blanchflower, Irish soccer midfielder (12 caps Northern Ireland; Manchester United 105 games; survived Munich air disaster), born in Belfast, Northern Ireland (d. 1998)
3rd largest snowfall then in NYC history (18.1")
The BBC broadcasts the news for the first time in color on television
Roman EmperorAntoninus Piusdies and is succeeded by co-EmperorsMarcus AureliusandLucius Verus, an unprecedented political arrangement in the Roman Empire
Peter Saur, fighting as Ray Steele, beatsBronko Nagurskiin St Louis, to become National Wrestling Association Heavyweight Champion
Magic Johnsonbecomes the second NBA player to reach 10,000 career assists
Peyton Manningannounces his retirement from the Denver Broncos and the NFL

Franz Graf von Pocci, German founding director of the Munich Marionette Theatre (Punch and Judy), poet, and illustrator of children's literature (Viola Tricolor: In Picture and Rhyme), born in Munich, Germany (d. 1876)
Maud Lewis (née Dowley), Canadian folk artist and painter, born in South Ohio, Nova Scotia (d. 1970)
Janet Collins, American ballerina, choreographer, and teacher, born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2003)
Olga Aleksandrovna Ladyzhenskaya, Russian mathematician (fluid dynamics), born in Kologriv, Soviet Russia (d. 2004)
Patrick "Paddy" Clancy, Irish folk singer, harmonic player, and songwriter (The Clancy Brothers and Tommy Makem - "Carrickfergus"), born in Carrick-on-Suir, County Tipperary, Ireland (d. 1998)
Rene Gagnon, US Marine shown in photograph of the Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima, born in Manchester, New Hampshire (d. 1979)
Yvonne Chouteau, Native American dancer (one of the "five Moons" of Oklahoma), born in Fort Worth, Texas (d. 2016)
David Baltimore, American biologist (1975 Nobel laureate in Physiology or Medicine), born in New York City
Chris White, British rock singer-songwriter, musician, and record producer (The Zombies - "She's Not There"; "Time Of The Season"), born in Barnet, Hertfordshire, England
Ernie Isley, American vocalist and guitarist (The Isley Brothers,1969-84 & 1991-present - "It's Your Thing"; "Heat is On"), born in Cincinnati, Ohio
